channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Quezon City Reports Rise in COVID-19 Cases Over Three Weeks
item.description: Quezon City experienced a significant surge in confirmed COVID-19 cases, rising from 23 to 84 in the past three weeks according to the city's epidemiology and surveillance unit. The data reveals that ...

item.title: AAP MP Sanjay Singh Alleges Political Bias in Ram Mandir Donation Probe
item.description: AAP Rajya Sabha MP Sanjay Singh has accused Vishva Hindu Parishad (VHP) president Alok Kumar of attempting to shield individuals involved in alleged financial irregularities linked to donations and co...

item.title: Ruto Defends Embu Votes, Emphasizes Long-standing Community Ties
item.description: President William Ruto has dismissed claims by former Deputy President Rigathi Gachagua that his support in Embu County was due to their political alliance, insisting his bond with the region was buil...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo Announces 2026 World Cup as Final Tournament
item.description: Portugal's Cristiano Ronaldo has confirmed that the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be his final appearance in the tournament, expressing his focus on enjoying the experience. The 41-year-old, speaking ahead...

item.title: UK's Next PM Faces Challenges in Managing Relations with Donald Trump
item.description: The article explores the challenges faced by Andy Burnham, the UK's next prime minister, in navigating complex relations with Donald Trump. It highlights expert advice on balancing diplomacy with firm...

item.title: Thunderstorm Flooding May Have Destroyed Family Home in Canora
item.description: A family home in Canora, Saskatchewan, may have been destroyed by severe thunderstorm flooding, according to local reports. The incident occurred on July 5, 2026, when intense weather conditions led t...

item.title: National Dialogue Steering Committee Faces Legal Challenge Over Appointment Process
item.description: The article details a legal challenge launched by nominees of South Africa's National Dialogue initiative against President Cyril Ramaphosa, alleging that the presidency failed to issue formal appoint...

item.title: The Cultural Significance of Stadium Music at the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup features carefully curated music playlists designed to enhance the stadium experience. FIFA’s stadium entertainment team collaborates with national associations to create cultu...

item.title: Lahore Police Arrest Suspects in Foreigners' Kidnap and Sexual Assault Case
item.description: Lahore police confirmed the involvement of a government minister's relative in the alleged abduction and sexual assault of two foreign women. The DIG Operations Kamran Faisal announced that all eight ...

item.title: Government Urges Public to Seek Medical Advice Only from Licensed Healthcare Professionals
item.description: The government has warned members of the public against relying on medical advice shared by unlicensed individuals on social media platforms. Health authorities said there has been a growing trend of ...

item.title: Former Partner of Journalist Lyra McKee Comments on Murder Case
item.description: Lyra McKee, a journalist from Belfast who recently moved to Derry, was fatally shot by a dissident republican gunman during rioting in Londonderry on 18 April 2019. Her former partner, Sara Canning, e...

item.title: Photo gallery captures key moments from the World Cup 2026 match between Mexico and England at the Azteca Stadium
item.description: This photo gallery showcases the standout moments from the FIFA World Cup 2026 match between Mexico and England at the iconic Azteca Stadium. The fixture, which was delayed by severe weather, carried ...

item.title: Clinton Advocates Electoral System Overhaul in the U.S.
item.description: Former U.S. Secretary of State Hillary Clinton has renewed calls for significant reforms to the American presidential election system, emphasizing the need for a more representative process. Her advoc...

item.title: Susanna Clarke reflects on illness, storytelling and the power of narrative
item.description: Susanna Clarke, author of 'The Invisible Library', shares her personal experience with chronic illness, drawing parallels between her journey and Virginia Woolf's essay. She explores how narratives sh...

item.title: President Ruto Criticizes MP Mukunji Over Political Allegiances and Development Focus
item.description: President William Ruto has publicly criticized Manyatta MP Gitonga Mukunji, accusing him of attempting to undermine his political authority and promoting divisive rhetoric instead of focusing on natio...

item.title: Senator Rodante Marcoleta Faces Plunder Charges in Anticorruption Court
item.description: Senator Rodante Marcoleta was arrested by the Sandiganbayan on July 6, 2026, following a non-bailable plunder case involving an alleged P75-million campaign donation. The court issued an arrest warran...

item.title: Border Matches in World Cup History: A Historical Overview
item.description: The article explores the frequency of border matches in FIFA World Cup history, highlighting Spain vs. Portugal as the first such encounter in 1930. It notes that 71 border matches have occurred acros...

item.title: Nuclear Regulatory Commission Proposes Easing Radiation Exposure Limits, Raising Health Concerns
item.description: Federal regulators are proposing to eliminate the ALARA standard, which aims to keep radiation exposure as low as reasonably achievable. This change would introduce a graded approach to radiation dose...

item.title: China to Conduct Nuclear-Capable Missile Test in South Pacific
item.description: China is preparing to test-fire a nuclear-capable long-range missile with a dummy warhead in the South Pacific within the next 24 hours, according to regional diplomats briefed by Chinese officials. T...
